data.text: EXCLUSIVE-US slows sale of Lukoil assets to pressure Russia in Ukraine peace talks
data.snippet: The United States has slowed the sale of Russian oil giant Lukoil's international assets to pressure Russia in the Ukraine peace talks, according to four sources familiar with the discussions.

image.text: A Lukoil sign is seen at Abu Dhabi International Progressive Energy Congress (ADIPEC), in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates, October 2, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: Lukoil's Litasco challenges Bulgaria over Russian crude import halt
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Switzerland-based Litasco, the trading arm of Russia's Lukoil, on Wednesday said measures adopted against it by Bulgaria breached the multilateral Energy Charter Treaty, and that it had submitted a notice of dispute against the country.

relatedHeadlines.text: Iraq approves 'amicable settlement' with Russia's Lukoil over transfer of operations of giant field
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The Iraqi cabinet approved on Tuesday an "amicable settlement" with Russia's Lukoil over the transfer of operations of the giant West Qurna 2 oil field to the state-run Basra Oil Company, according to a statement.

data.text: EU seeks G7 coordination on maritime services ban on Russian seaborne oil
data.snippet: The European Union needs to coordinate its plan to impose a full maritime services ban on Russia's seaborne crude oil exports with other G7 countries before pushing ahead with the measure, the EU's sanctions envoy said on Thursday.

image.text: Rosneft's Russian-flagged crude oil tanker Vladimir Monomakh transits the Bosphorus in Istanbul, Turkey, July 6, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: Russia plans to divert more oil revenues to budget reserve fund
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Russia plans to divert more oil revenues into the budget reserve fund to preserve it from depletion and to take pressure off the currency market, where the rouble is strengthening, Finance Minister Anton Siluanov said on Wednesday.

relatedHeadlines.text: EXCLUSIVE-Russia's Transneft cuts pipeline oil intake after drone attack on key pumping station, sources say
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Russia's oil pipeline monopoly Transneft has cut crude intake into its system by some 250,000 barrels per day after a Ukrainian drone attack on a key pumping station serving major oil hubs and ports on Monday, two sources familiar with the situation said on Tuesday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Russia's seaborne exports of oil products may fall 10% in February

data.text: Ukraine's grain deliveries to ports rise in February, railway says
data.snippet: Ukraine's grain deliveries to its Black Sea ports for export have risen by 2% so far in February compared with January, but volumes remain below February 2025 levels, state railway Ukrzaliznytsia said on Thursday.

image.text: A cargo vessel leaves a port in the Black Sea, amid Russia's attack on Ukraine, in Odesa, Ukraine March 26, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Ukrainian export prices for new rapeseed crop rise amid fears of smaller output, union says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Indicative export prices for Ukraine's 2026 rapeseed harvest have risen to $530-$540 per ton CPT July-August delivery from $510-$520 reported a month earlier, as weather problems could reduce the harvest, farmers' union UAC said on Wednesday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Ukraine grain sowing may be delayed by deeply frozen soil, chief forecaster says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Record low temperatures across most of Ukraine in February led to deep soil freezing, which could delay the start of the grain sowing campaign, the head of the agricultural department of the state meteorological centre was quoted as saying late on Monday.

relatedHeadlines.text: Ukraine sees higher wheat, corn harvest and exports in 2025/26
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Ukraine's wheat harvest may grow by 2.9% to 23.1 million metric tons in 2026 due to an increase in sowing acreage and despite a decline in yield, the UCAB agricultural lobby and the economy ministry said over the weekend.

data.text: Oil prices little changed in choppy trade as traders follow US, Iran talks
data.snippet: Oil prices were little changed in choppy trade on Thursday as investors tracked developments in talks between the U.S. and Iran over the OPEC member's nuclear program, weighing potential supply concerns if hostilities escalate.

image.text: Oil platforms and pumpjacks at Lake Maracaibo, in Cabimas, Venezuela, January 26, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: US crude inventories rise the most in 3 years as refinery demand slips, imports increase, EIA says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: U.S. crude inventories rose by the most last week since February 2023, as refinery utilization slowed and imports increased, the Energy Information Administration said on Wednesday.

relatedHeadlines.text: UBS sees modest oil price pullback if mideast tensions don't disrupt supply
relatedHeadlines.snippet: UBS on Tuesday said it expects a modest decline in oil prices in coming weeks provided there is no escalation of tensions in the Middle East that could disrupt supply.

relatedHeadlines.text: North Dakota operators likely to keep crude output steady this year, regulator says

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Trump EPA to shift at least half of waived biofuel obligations to big refiners, sources say
data.snippet: The Trump administration has settled on a plan that would require big oil refineries to make up for at least half of the biofuel blending volumes obligations waived in recent years under the Small Refinery Exemption program, according to three sources familiar with the discussions.

image.text: Signage at the headquarters of the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) in Washington, D.C., U.S., February 18, 2025.

data.text: Russia eyes higher Urals oil exports to China amid Indian cutbacks, traders say
data.snippet: Russia will aim to keep its Urals crude oil exports steady in March by boosting shipments to China as India, a key buyer, cuts purchases following a trade deal with the U.S.

image.text: An oil tanker (R) is being pushed towards a storage tanker at the Belokamenka Oil Terminal in Kolsky Bay near the northwestern Russian port city of Murmansk June 19, 2006.

relatedHeadlines.text: Four years into war, Russia's energy revenues drop but oil keeps flowing
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The money Russia earned from exporting oil and gas dropped over the last 12 months, even as the country's oil exports increased in volume, according to data released on Tuesday, the fourth anniversary of Moscow's full-scale invasion of Ukraine.

relatedHeadlines.text: China's Russian oil imports to hit new record in February as India cuts back
relatedHeadlines.snippet: China's Russian oil imports are set to climb for a third straight month to a new record high in February as independent refiners snapped up deeply discounted cargoes after India slashed purchases, according to traders and ship-tracking data.

relatedHeadlines.text: India's Russian oil imports down in January amid trade talks with US
relatedHeadlines.snippet: India's Russian oil imports slipped in January, continuing a downturn that began in December, as refiners sought more alternative barrels under Western sanctions pressure and ongoing U.S.–India trade talks, Reuters sources said and data showed.

data.text: Asia prices of LPG, naphtha jump after Saudi terminal outage
data.snippet: Prices of liquefied petroleum gas and naphtha jumped in Asia on Thursday after Saudi Aramco halted exports from a key terminal, disrupting supplies to the region, according to trade sources and LSEG data, with India likely to take the biggest hit.

image.text: A view shows Saudi Aramco's Juaymah facility, Saudi Arabia November 26, 2013.

relatedHeadlines.text: Aramco cancels Juaymah LPG deliveries through March for repairs, traders say
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Saudi Aramco's Juaymah liquefied petroleum gas export terminal is expected to be offline for repairs for at least a month after an outage this week forced the cancellation of cargoes through March, traders said on Thursday.

data.text: Hungary's Orban asks EU to go verify Druzhba pipeline damage
data.snippet: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban on Wednesday asked the EU for a "fact-finding mission" to assess damage to the Druzhba oil pipeline in Ukraine, and suggested this could help unblock new EU funding to Ukraine, a letter seen by Reuters showed.

image.text: The receiver station of the Druzhba oil pipeline between Hungary and Russia is seen at the Hungarian MOL Group's Danube Refinery in Szazhalombatta, Hungary, May 18, 2022.

relatedHeadlines.text: EXPLAINER-What is the Druzhba oil pipeline and why has it held up EU sanctions on Russia?
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The stoppage of the last Russian oil flows to Europe, via the Soviet-era Druzhba pipeline, has triggered a geopolitical clash that has blocked new EU sanctions on Russia and vital funding for Ukraine.

relatedHeadlines.text: Russia accuses Ukraine of threatening European energy security with Hungary/Slovakia oil stoppage
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova on Thursday accused Ukraine of threatening Europe's energy security by stopping Russian oil flowing through the Druzhba oil pipeline to Hungary and Slovakia.

relatedHeadlines.text: Croatia assessing legality of importing Russian oil, EU says
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Croatia is assessing whether it can lawfully import seaborne Russian crude oil to supply to Hungary and Slovakia after the Druzhba pipeline supplying them via Ukraine was damaged, the European Commission said on Wednesday.

data.text: Mideast-Asia oil tanker rates at highest since 2020 as Iran tensions simmer
data.snippet: The cost of hiring a supertanker from the Middle East to China exceeded $200,000 a day on Thursday for the first time since 2020 as the threat of U.S. attacks on Iran grows and buyers seek to lock in oil cargoes, according to data and market sources.

image.text: An oil tanker is seen in Umm Al Quwain, United Arab Emirates, February 8, 2021.

relatedHeadlines.text: Middle East oil exports push tanker costs to 6-year-high amid threat of US-Iran war
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The cost of shipping oil has surged to the highest in six years, fuelled by a wave of crude exports from the Middle East as traders accelerate charters ahead of possible military conflict between the U.S. and Iran, industry sources said.

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Ivory Coast to cut farmer cocoa price with earlier start of mid-crop, sources say
data.snippet: Ivory Coast plans to bring forward the start of its cocoa mid-crop season for the first time ever, allowing the regulator to sharply reduce its set price paid to farmers in hopes of boosting sales, two government sources and two regulator sources told Reuters.

image.text: Kouassi Kouassi Frederic, a cocoa farmer, holds cocoa pods at a farm in the village of Remikro in Fengolo, Ivory Coast, February 11, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: EXPLAINER-How did Ivory Coast and Ghana's cocoa sales crisis come about?
relatedHeadlines.snippet: The producers of half the world's cocoa - Ivory Coast and Ghana - have struggled to sell beans and pay farmers this year due to ample global harvests, lower cocoa prices and falling demand from chocolate makers for the ingredient.

relatedHeadlines.text: Ivory Coast unsold cocoa stocks set to soar if price standoff persists
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Ivory Coast will have accumulated about 200,000 metric tons of unsold cocoa by end-March when its main crop concludes unless the government cuts state-regulated farmer prices in order to unlock sales from farmers to traders, industry experts and global trading executives said.

data.text: ROI-Geopolitical turmoil gives OPEC+ cover for cautious output hike: Bousso
data.snippet: OPEC+ meets on Sunday to set its next production move amid heightened geopolitical friction affecting nearly a third of its output. That volatility may give the group cover to stick to its balanced-market narrative, even if the facts on the ground suggest otherwise.

image.text: A model of an oil pump is seen in front of the OPEC logo in this illustration taken January 9, 2026.

relatedHeadlines.text: OPEC+ to consider 137,000 bpd oil output increase for April, sources say
relatedHeadlines.snippet: OPEC+ is likely to consider raising its oil output by 137,000 barrels per day for April, three sources with knowledge of OPEC+ thinking said as the group prepares for peak summer demand and a price boost from tensions between the U.S. and OPEC member Iran.

relatedHeadlines.text: OPEC sees world demand for OPEC+ crude falling in second quarter
relatedHeadlines.snippet: OPEC on Wednesday forecast world oil demand for crude from the wider OPEC+ producer group will drop by 400,000 barrels per day in the second quarter from the first three months of this year, a copy of its monthly oil report seen by Reuters shows.

data.text: ROI-China is shifting crude oil buying in response to price rally: Russell
data.snippet: One of the least talked about dynamics of the global crude oil market is the role China plays in setting a floor and a ceiling for prices.

image.text: An aerial view shows a crude oil tanker at an oil terminal off Waidiao island in Zhoushan, Zhejiang province, China January 4, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: ROI-China accelerates crude stockpiling amid weaker oil price trend: Russell
relatedHeadlines.snippet: China's flows of crude oil into storage probably jumped in November to the highest in six months, as a surge in imports overwhelmed steady refinery processing rates.

data.text: India braces for unusually hot March; wheat, rapeseed crops at risk, sources say
data.snippet: India is likely to record one of its warmest Marches on record, with above-average temperatures forecast in key wheat and rapeseed-growing states, potentially cutting yields, two weather bureau sources said on Thursday.

image.text: A farmer works in a wheat field near Jaipur, India, February 24, 2023.

data.text: Engie shares surge on $14 billion UK power grid deal
data.snippet: Shares in French utility Engie surged on Thursday after it said it would buy major British grid company UK Power Networks for $14 billion, marking its largest ever deal and a return to acquisitions after several years of selling off non-core assets.

image.text: The Engie logo is displayed during the 107th session of the Congress of Mayors organised by the "France's Mayors' Association" (AMF) at the Paris Expo Porte de Versailles convention center in Paris, France, November 19, 2025.

relatedHeadlines.text: Italy's Eni beats profit forecast on strong upstream business
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Italian energy group Eni beat forecasts on Thursday with a 35% year-on-year jump in fourth-quarter adjusted earnings, driven by a strong performance in its exploration and production division and improved refining results.

relatedHeadlines.text: Cheniere lifts buyback plan to above $10 billion as profit surges on strong LNG demand
relatedHeadlines.snippet: Cheniere Energy said its fourth-quarter profit more than doubled on strong liquefied natural gas (LNG) demand and the company raised its share repurchase target to more than $10 billion through 2030.

data.text: EXCLUSIVE-Rare earth shortages worsen in US aerospace, chips despite trade truce, sources say
data.snippet: S uppliers to U.S. aerospace and semiconductor firms face worsening rare earth shortages, with two turning away some clients, industry insiders said, weeks before U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to meet his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping for a summit in Beijing.

image.text: Semiconductor chips are seen on a printed circuit board in this illustration picture taken February 17, 2023.

relatedHeadlines.text: ROI-West needs its own pricing to escape China's rare earths grip: Andy Home
relatedHeadlines.snippet: A sharp rally in the price of rare earths has propelled the market above the floor price guaranteed by the U.S. government in its ground-breaking deal with domestic producer MP Materials.

notifications.message: Your requested language with value en-GB is not supported, using en-us instead.
